primitives.name: attention
primitives.blurb: Lets every position look at every other position and mix in what's relevant. Comes in self- (within one sequence), cross- (between two), and multi-head (many in parallel) flavors.

primitives.name: feed-forward (MLP)
primitives.blurb: A small per-position neural net applied identically everywhere; where transformers store much of their factual knowledge.

primitives.name: convolution
primitives.blurb: Slides a small learned filter across the input, so nearby pixels are processed together and the same pattern is detected anywhere.

primitives.name: recurrence
primitives.blurb: Processes a sequence one step at a time, carrying a hidden state forward — memory as a running summary.

primitives.name: tokenization
primitives.blurb: Chops raw text into the integer IDs models actually see (BPE, WordPiece) — the layer where spelling, counting, and multilingual quirks live.

primitives.name: embedding
primitives.blurb: Turns discrete tokens (words, image patches) into vectors of numbers the network can work with.

primitives.name: normalization
primitives.blurb: Rescales activations so training stays stable as networks get deep.

primitives.name: residual connection
primitives.blurb: Adds a layer's input back to its output, giving gradients a highway through deep stacks.

primitives.name: positional encoding
primitives.blurb: Injects “where am I in the sequence” information, since attention alone is order-blind.

primitives.name: state-space scan
primitives.blurb: A linear dynamical system run along the sequence — recurrence you can compute in parallel; the heart of Mamba/S4.

primitives.name: gating / routing
primitives.blurb: Learned switches deciding what flows where — the gates inside an LSTM cell, the router picking experts in MoE.

modules.name: transformer block
modules.blurb: The repeating unit: attention then feed-forward, each wrapped in a residual connection and normalization. Stack N to get an encoder or decoder.

modules.name: encoder
modules.blurb: Reads the whole input at once (bidirectionally) and produces a contextual representation — the “understanding” half.

modules.name: decoder
modules.blurb: Generates output one token at a time, each step attending only to what came before — the “generation” half.

modules.name: predictor
modules.blurb: A lightweight module that predicts the representation of hidden content from visible context — the defining piece of JEPA and latent world models.

modules.name: denoiser
modules.blurb: The network inside diffusion that looks at a noisy input and predicts what to remove. Its backbone is swappable — that's where DiT lives.

modules.name: generator / discriminator
modules.blurb: A forger and a detective trained against each other: one synthesizes samples, the other judges real vs fake.

modules.name: output head
modules.blurb: The final projection from hidden vectors to actual predictions — logits over a vocabulary, class scores, values, or actions.

modifiers.name: Mixture of Experts
modifiers.blurb: Swaps the dense feed-forward block for many routed “expert” MLPs — only a few fire per token. A modifier on a block, not a peer architecture.

modifiers.name: head sharing (GQA/MLA)
modifiers.blurb: Shares key/value projections across query heads to shrink the KV cache at inference. A modifier on attention, not a peer architecture.

objectives.name: autoregressive
objectives.blurb: predict the next token/frame given everything so far

objectives.name: masked
objectives.blurb: hide pieces of the input and reconstruct them from context

objectives.name: contrastive
objectives.blurb: pull matched pairs together in embedding space, push mismatches apart

objectives.name: denoising
objectives.blurb: predict the noise that was added to the data, step by step

objectives.name: adversarial
objectives.blurb: a generator tries to fool a discriminator in a minimax game

objectives.name: reconstruction
objectives.blurb: squeeze the input through a bottleneck and rebuild it

objectives.name: joint-embedding
objectives.blurb: predict the representation of hidden content, not its pixels or tokens

objectives.name: flow matching
objectives.blurb: regress the velocity field that transports noise into data

objectives.name: energy
objectives.blurb: assign low energy to real data, high to everything else

families.name: LLM
families.blurb: A large model trained to predict the next token; the dominant form stacks decoder blocks at enormous scale (GPT, Claude, Llama).
families.variants: “LLM” is a category, not one architecture: T5/UL2 are encoder–decoder LLMs; Mamba/Jamba/RWKV are non-transformer LLMs. The invariant is the autoregressive objective at scale.
families.differentiator: answers from weights alone — everything it knows was baked in at training

families.name: BERT (encoder-only)
families.blurb: A stack of bidirectional encoder blocks trained to fill in masked words — built to understand text, not to generate it.

families.name: encoder–decoder
families.blurb: An encoder reads the source; a decoder generates the target while cross-attending to it — the original Transformer wiring (translation, T5, BART).
families.variants: Decoding is autoregressive, but pretraining varies: seq2seq next-token (translation) or span-denoising (T5/BART).

families.name: Vision Transformer (ViT)
families.blurb: Chops an image into patches, embeds each like a token, and runs a standard encoder over them. Trained supervised, masked (MAE), or contrastive — the objective is not fixed.

families.name: CNN
families.blurb: Stacks of convolution filters with pooling — local pattern detectors composed into global understanding; the vision default before ViT (ResNet, EfficientNet).

families.name: RNN / LSTM
families.blurb: Processes sequences step by step with a carried hidden state; LSTM adds gates so the memory survives long sequences. The pre-transformer default for language.

families.name: diffusion
families.blurb: Generate by reversing noise: start from static and repeatedly denoise until an image (or video) emerges. The denoiser inside can be a U-Net or a DiT.

families.name: GAN
families.blurb: A generator forges samples while a discriminator learns to spot fakes; trained against each other until the forgeries get good (StyleGAN).

families.name: VAE
families.blurb: An autoencoder whose bottleneck is a probability distribution — rebuild the input, but keep the latent space smooth enough to sample new data from (ELBO = reconstruction + KL).
families.differentiator: the latent is a probability distribution you can sample new data from

families.name: CLIP (dual-encoder)
families.blurb: Two separate encoders — one for images, one for text — trained so matching pairs land close together in a shared embedding space (CLIP, SigLIP).
families.variants: Not a single tower: two encoders trained jointly. The image encoder is typically a ViT; the text encoder a transformer.

families.name: autoencoder
families.blurb: Encoder squeezes the input through a bottleneck; decoder rebuilds it. The bottleneck is forced to learn what matters (parent of VAE and sparse autoencoders).
families.differentiator: the plain version — compress through a bottleneck and rebuild, nothing else

families.name: VQ-VAE
families.blurb: An autoencoder with a discrete codebook bottleneck — images become grids of tokens, which is what lets transformers generate them (VQGAN, DALL-E 1).
families.differentiator: the latent snaps to a discrete codebook entry — tokens for images

families.name: JEPA
families.blurb: Predict the representation of hidden content rather than its pixels — LeCun's alternative to generative pretraining (I-JEPA, V-JEPA).

families.name: MAE
families.blurb: Hide 75% of an image's patches and train a ViT to reconstruct them — BERT's fill-in-the-blank trick applied to pixels (MAE, BEiT).

families.name: SSM / Mamba
families.blurb: Replaces attention with a selective state-space scan — recurrent memory you can train in parallel, sub-quadratic in sequence length. Mostly used autoregressively (Mamba, S4).

families.name: GNN
families.blurb: Message passing over a graph: each node repeatedly aggregates its neighbors' vectors. The aggregation itself is built from the same MLP primitives (GCN, GAT).

families.name: Perceiver
families.blurb: Cross-attend any-size input into a small fixed latent array, then run the transformer there — one architecture for images, audio, point clouds (Perceiver IO).

families.name: flow matching
families.blurb: Like diffusion, but learn a straight-line velocity field from noise to data instead of stepwise denoising — fewer sampling steps, same backbones (SD3, Flux).
families.differentiator: learns a velocity field along any noise-to-data path

families.name: rectified flow
families.blurb: Flow matching with deliberately straightened noise-to-data paths — few-step or even one-step sampling (SD3, Flux).
families.differentiator: straightens that path so generation takes only a few steps

families.name: energy-based
families.blurb: A network assigns a scalar “energy” to configurations — low for real data, high otherwise; generation is descent down the energy landscape (EBMs, Hopfield nets).

families.name: VLM
families.blurb: A vision encoder feeding (through a projector) into an LLM decoder — the dominant way to make a language model see (LLaVA, Flamingo).
families.variants: A composition, not a new wiring: vision encoder (often a ViT) + projector + LLM decoder. Some VLMs interleave cross-attention instead (Flamingo).
families.differentiator: a vision encoder feeds image tokens to a language decoder that talks about them

families.name: VLA
families.blurb: A VLM extended for robotics: see the scene, read the instruction, output motor actions as tokens (RT-2, π0).
families.differentiator: same wiring, but the decoder outputs robot actions instead of just text

families.name: RAG
families.blurb: Bolt a search step onto a generator — fetch relevant documents at inference time and condition on them instead of memorizing everything in weights (RAG, RETRO).
families.differentiator: bolts a retriever on — fetches documents into context at inference time

families.name: world model
families.blurb: Learns the latent dynamics of an environment so an agent can predict and plan — “what happens next if I act?” (Dreamer, Genie).
families.variants: A goal, not one wiring: Genie is a diffusion world model; Dreamer is RNN/SSM-based; JEPA-style latent prediction is a third route.
